3. Understand the role of digital technology in media production and

consumption.

4. Demonstrate written and oral communication skills.

5. Recognize ethical guidelines and laws that journalists adhere to while

reporting and publishing news.

REQUIREMENTS The Media, Technology and Democracy minor is a total of 15 credits, including one required course (JOUR200, Journalism History, Roles and Structures), and four approved journalism electives, one of which would come from the 100 to 200 level. A grade of C- or better is required in all minor courses. Note: Additional courses beyond those listed below may be added later by the college to satisfy the 12 credits of electives.
